seo.title: Full Cycle Health | Sophia Bush Anderson | Wolfville, NS
seo.description: Full Cycle Health offers evidence-based fertility awareness education and menstrual cycle charting with Sophia Bush Anderson in Wolfville, Nova Scotia and online. Private coaching and small-group programs.

hero.eyebrow: Full Cycle Health · Fertility awareness education
hero.headline: Body literacy for every body
hero.paragraph1: We teach evidence-based menstrual cycle charting through private coaching and small-group programs—so you can read your fertile signs, care for your health, and make informed choices about contraception, conception, from menarche to menopause.
hero.paragraph2: Led by Fertility Awareness Educator Sophia Bush-Anderson, in person in Wolfville, Nova Scotia and online. From your first period through midlife transitions—including coming off hormonal birth control—you build real skill, not guesswork.

wisdom.heading: Your body has its own individual ecology
wisdom.body1Html: This approach invites you to become the expert on your own experience. By exploring the terrain of your body—its boundaries, patterns, and points of change—you begin to understand what is normal for you.
wisdom.body2: With this data, you can recognize the patterns of each season and spot the signs of dis-ease. This is about deep self-understanding and true agency.
wisdom.quote: “There is no reason why women shouldn’t have as much information about their bodies as physicians do.”

pathwaysSection.heading: Where are you in your journey?
pathwaysSection.intro: Different seasons of life require different maps. Whether you are navigating health transitions or seeking reproductive autonomy, we provide the framework to navigate your cycle with confidence.

pathways.title: Breaking up with the Pill
pathways.description: Move beyond "shutting down" your system. Gain the tools to understand your actual risks and make informed reproductive choices.

pathways.title: Mastering your cycle
pathways.description: Stop guessing and start knowing. Gain an evidence-based system for deep self-understanding and sexual empowerment.

pathways.title: Navigating Perimenopause
pathways.description: Shaky ground becomes stable through observation. Charting helps identify where you are in the journey and empowers you to manage shifts.

pathways.title: Conscious Conception
pathways.description: Your cycle is a health report card. Use diagnostic charting to restore your terrain and understand your specific window of opportunity.

about.name: Sophia Bush Anderson
about.heading: Empowerment through Body Literacy.
about.bio1: I’m Sophia Bush Anderson. I teach body literacy so that people can understand their cycles, health, and choices with more clarity and agency. Learning the language of our own bodies helps us be in better relationship to ourselves—and to one another.
about.bio2: My teaching is still shaped by years as a dance artist and Pilates instructor, and by a commitment to justice that runs through my studies in international development, sustainability, and community development at Dalhousie and Acadia Universities.
about.trainingHtml:
In 2023 I enrolled at Justisse College International in a two-year, master’s-level program to become a Holistic Reproductive Health Practitioner (HRHP) and a Fertility Awareness Educator (FAE). The training is rigorous and full-time, moving from the social and cultural history of contraception to reproductive endocrinology and physiology, and through to the foundations of holistic health. After a year of maternity leave, I am entering my supervised clinical practicum beginning April 1.

You can read more about Justisse College and its programs here. I am also a member of the Association of Fertility Awareness Professionals (AFAP).
about.pullQuote: This work is for anyone who wants their pregnancy, birth, and parenting to be consensual.
about.valuesFooter: Based in Wolfville, Nova Scotia, Full Cycle Health serves clients locally and online, providing a holistic view of the cycle as a vital part of personal ecology.

faqSection.heading: Common Questions
faqSection.intro: Clarifications on the method and how we work together.

items.question: How will we meet?
items.answerHtml: We offer flexible meeting options to fit your life. Sessions are available both online via secure video conferencing or in person at our local practice space in Wolfville, Nova Scotia, Canada.

items.question: How do I know this is for me?
items.answerHtml: Ask yourself: Do you want to learn more about your body? Do you want to know how to prevent pregnancy effectively with just your own detective skills? Do you want to avoid hormonal disruptors or achieve pregnancy more quickly? If you want to improve your menstrual health or understand the seasons of your cycle, you're in the right place.

items.question: What's involved in the learning process?
items.answerHtml: We go deep into reproductive anatomy, the mechanisms of the menstrual cycle, and primary signs of fertility. You'll learn to observe these in your body daily and chart them according to the Justisse Method. This may also involve lifestyle or diet changes depending on your goals.

items.question: What if I'm going through perimenopause?
items.answerHtml: You're right on time. Charting can help you make sense of the perimenopausal transition, understand where you are in it, and empower you with knowledge to take meaningful action—whether for health maintenance or reproductive goals.

items.question: I'm tracking my period with an app. How is this different?
items.answerHtml: An app uses calculations to estimate when you might ovulate based on your past. This systematic method is about checking in real-time with your day-to-day changes. It's about getting closer to your body and confirming ovulation accurately.

items.question: What if I'm currently using a hormonal contraceptive?
items.answerHtml: You can still learn how to chart! While hormonal methods suppress biomarkers, starting the education now prepares you for the transition. Let's do a discovery call so I can find out more about your intentions.

items.question: Are you associated with a church or religious organization?
items.answerHtml: No. Fertility Awareness-Based Methods are split into two groups. Natural Family Planning (NFP) methods are religious, while Fertility Awareness Methods (FAM) are secular. The Justisse Method is the largest secular FABM in the world.

offeringsSection.heading: Learning Journeys
offeringsSection.individualIntro: Programs for people who want to learn charting for themselves—whether you thrive in a small group or prefer private coaching at your own pace.
offeringsSection.groupIntro: Workshops for schools, community organizations, and individuals who want Sophia to facilitate body literacy for their students, members, or groups.

individualCards.title: Private coaching package
individualCards.tagline: Eight one-on-one sessions tailored to your goals.
individualCards.descriptionHtml:
Whatever your reproductive goals—whether you are TTC or TTA—this is for you if you want a secular, evidence-based path toward agency, empowerment, and the freedom that comes from truly understanding your body.

A private container where we learn how to chart for health, cycle literacy, reproductive sovereignty, and the practical habits that help your chart tell a clearer story.

This option fits especially well when you want more flexibility, room to move at your own pace, space to bring a partner, focused support through conception delays or health questions, or a private setting where you can go as deep as you need without an audience.

groupCards.title: Period Positivity Workshop
groupCards.descriptionHtml:
A lively, educational body-literacy workshop for youth ages 8–12: participants build the knowledge and language they need to relate to their period and cycle with confidence, curiosity, and care.

The offering includes a three-hour facilitated session, all materials, and a take-home period pouch for each participant. Past partners include Family Resource Centres, Girl Guides, the Young Women’s Christian Association (YWCA), and the Town of Wolfville.
groupCards.priceNote: Take-home period pouch for every participant.

groupCards.title: Perimenopause Unlocked
groupCards.descriptionHtml:
This lab is built to demystify perimenopause and normalize what can feel like a lonely, confusing stretch. We cover hormonal and physiological patterns in plain language and translate that understanding into practical strategies for health, empowerment, and self-care.

The session is three hours and is intended for people who cycle, ages 35–55—whether you are starting to notice changes or already navigating a bumpier season and want grounded information you can use right away.
groupCards.priceNote: Final fee depends on group size and format; we confirm details together before you book.

groupCards.title: Fertility Awareness 101
groupCards.descriptionHtml:
Want to learn more about fertility awareness? Sophia can join your community for an accessible primer on what fertility awareness-based methods are, how they work in real bodies (not just in theory), and what charting with intention can offer.

There is time for honest questions, myth-checking, and conversation—and you will leave with curated resources so your group feels informed, oriented, and supported rather than overwhelmed.
groupCards.priceSecondary: We set a fair rate together for your organization or community.

contactSection.heading: Ready to claim your reproductive agency?
contactSection.intro: Send a note and Sophia will be in touch personally. Whether you're curious about a program, weighing a discovery call, or looking for a workshop for your community — start here.
topicOptions: I'd like to book a discovery call
I'm interested in a program for myself
I'd like a workshop for my organization
contactSection.messagePlaceholder: A few sentences about where you're at and what you're hoping to explore.
contactSection.reassurance: I read every message personally and reply within two business days.
contactSection.successHeadline: Thank you — your message is on its way.
contactSection.successBody: I'll be in touch within two business days. In the meantime, take a deep breath and know that reaching out is itself a powerful first step.

footer.mission: Teaching body literacy as a tool for empowerment, safety, and liberation.
footer.locationSub: From your first period to your last.
footer.copyrightAfterYear: Full Cycle Health & Fertility Awareness. Sophia Bush Anderson.
